observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to follow the instructions set forth under Annex 49 of the Manual in accomplishing the RPCPPE and after effecting aforesaid changes, submit the revised RPCPPE of the City as of December 31, 2023 to the Audit Team for further verification and audit; b. Instruct the City Accountant to prepare necessary accounting entries to reclassify the semi-expendable PPE to the appropriate semi-expendable property account; and c. At the earliest and practicable period, initiate the one-time cleansing of PPE by following the guidelines and procedures on the six activities prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly applying the prescribed guidelines on the inventory, reconciliation of records and disposition for missing and unaccounted PPE.
observations.managementAction: a. Instruct the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to follow the instructions set forth under Annex 49 of the Manual in accomplishing the RPCPPE and after effecting aforesaid changes, submit the revised RPCPPE of the City as of December 31, 2023 to the Audit Team for further verification and audit; b. Instruct the City Accountant to prepare necessary accounting entries to reclassify the semi-expendable PPE to the appropriate semi-expendable property account; c. At the earliest and practicable period, initiate the one-time cleansing of PPE by following the guidelines and procedures on the six activities prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly applying the prescribed guidelines on the inventory, reconciliation of records and disposition for missing and unaccounted PPE.
observations.reason: The City has yet to avail and implement the procedures of the One-time Cleansing of PPE account balances provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Ensure that the APP of the City for CY 2024 and succeeding years are in accordance with the requirements on the preparation of the APP under GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 and bear approval of the Head of Procuring Entity following Section 7.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA No. 9184, and submit the accompanying document to the COA Audit Team as the Annual Budget; b. Ensure significant accomplishment of procurement activities considering funds are available thereto; and c. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to report all bid results, regardless of modality, in the Report of Bid Results submitted and uploaded in the Full Disclosure Policy Portal (FDPP) and henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related issuances regarding procurement and the preparation of relevant plans and documents.
observations.managementAction: a. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairperson submitted the CY 2025 APP of the City in the format pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 07-2015. b. The City through its BAC submitted the PMR (Procurement Monitoring Report). c. The City submitted and uploaded in the FDPP the Report of Bid Results for CY 2024. However, there were variances in posted quantities and amounts.
observations.reason: The PMR submitted by the BAC consisted of 2,019 procurement activities, with a total amount of P1,027,593,020.28. However, the posted quantity and amount of procurement activities in the FDPP were 181 and P820,371,956.98, respectively, indicating a variance in quantity and amount of 1,838 and P207,221,063.30, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Subject all deliveries of all items for acceptance first by the CGSO before inspection to ensure proper and accurate recording, inventory and monitoring; and b. Duly notify the COA Audit Team of the time and scheduled delivery date of all purchases of the City within 24 hours from such acceptance and submit the corresponding report of inspection thereafter; and henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 92-386 and 95-006 and the Manual relative to the acceptance and inspection of the purchases of the City and the notification of delivery and submission of the report of inspection to COA.
observations.managementAction: a. Deliveries of the City in CY 2024 were accepted by either the City Treasurer, the City Administrator and the respective end-user/s. b. The City Treasurer’s Office started notifying the Audit Team regarding the City’s purchases in CY 2025. The City has started subjecting deliveries of the City for acceptance first by the CGSO before inspection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor, through the Head of the City Human Resource Management Office (CHRMO), expedite the hiring and/or promotion of qualified personnel to reduce, if not totally eliminate, the need to appoint casual and JO workers, thus ensuring a more efficient and effective government service thru strategic management of human resources.
observations.managementAction: The City consistently complied with the hiring and/or promotion of qualified employees to fill up the total vacant positions. The City’s Human Resource and Management Office conducted hiring and/or promotion activities.
observations.reason: Filling-up of positions are still in-progress as of report date; the total number of casual employees and JOs increased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Require the Inventory Committee to classify the PPE under Special Education Fund to their respective PPE account and categorized classification or category; b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to exert all efforts to determine the Accountable Officers responsible or liable for the unaccounted PPE, if any; and c. Avail of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, particularly applying the prescribed guidelines on the inventory, reconciliation of records and disposition for missing and unaccounted PPE.
observations.managementAction: a. The submitted RPCPPE of the City as of December 31, 2024 reported PPE correctly classified. b. The City conducted investigations and retrieved accountability records on file, determining the Accountable Officers responsible for the unaccounted PPE. c. No action taken by Management to avail the One-Time Cleansing of PPE procedures.
observations.reason: The City has yet to avail and implement the One-Time Cleansing of PPE as per COA Circular No. 2020-006.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:

a. The City Accountant to:

i. refrain from recording the procurement of equipment directly to expense;

ii. prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the purchases totaling
P5,582,934.00 to the appropriate PPE accounts and furnish the Audit Team
of the JEV and supporting documents, for verification and audit;

iii. in coordination with the City General Services Officer, submit the
corresponding PAR for the subject purchases of equipment to the Audit
Team pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001; and

b. The City Accountant and the City General Services Officer to strictly adhere to
COA Circular No. 2020-006 and related issuances, regarding the issuance and
recording of PPE.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to conduct timely
reconciliations of their records on expendable properties, ensuring that the
reported figures agree regardless of the cut-off date;

b. Instruct the City Accountant to submit the following documents and strictly
adhere to the provisions of PD No. 1445 and the MNGAS for LGUs regarding
the accounting and reporting of inventories:

i. corrected SL of the Other Supplies and Materials Inventory as of December
31, 2024 under the SEF; and

ii. JEVs and SSMIs to the Audit Team pertaining to the utilization of
expendable properties during the year totaling P354,089,613.85.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to:

a. Submit immediately the corresponding JEVs, approved DVs, perfected
contract, AIR, and/or notice of the existence of check/s pertaining to the
recorded Accounts Payable amounting to P92,880,447.85 to the Audit Team;
otherwise, make the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify, revert or
derecognize, whichever is applicable, and then submit the corresponding JEVs
to the Audit Team, for verification and audit;

b. Refrain from recognizing Accounts Payable unless the required documents to
establish the validity of the claims are complete pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD
No. 1445; and

c. Properly classify liabilities based on the description of the accounts under
COA Circular No. 2015-009 and henceforth, strictly comply with the submission
of JEVs and supporting documents to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 31,
Chapter 2 of the MNGAS for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Instruct the City Accountant to process the JEV recording the double payment
in the Due to BIR account and submit the same to the Audit Team for
verification; and

b. Prospectively, ensure accurate remittance of taxes withheld pursuant to BIR
RMC No. 23-2012 and related issuances.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to strictly adhere
with the provisions of PD No. 1445, COA Circular Nos. 96-011 and 2009-006, and
the MNGAS for LGUs Volume I regarding the timely and complete submission of
monthly BRS and Pre-Closing Trial Balances.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Ensure that the recipients/beneficiaries of the financial assistance, regardless
of type, are identified or determined before the grant of the cash advance, to
avoid huge amounts of cash advance refunds;

b. Require the preparation of the ObR as an integral part of the DV rather than an
attachment to the liquidation documents, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2006-
002; and

c. Direct the Accountable Officers to refrain from consolidating payments for
different purposes into a single cash advance following Item 4.1.1 of COA
Circular No. 97-002 and henceforth, strictly adhere to the guidelines on the
granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances under COA Circular No.
97-002.

We recommended that the City Mayor and Chairman of the LSB direct the LSB to
include the amount of P215,218,687.79 in the CY 2025 SEF Budget and present the
line items therein by PPA, allotment class and per school following Item 5.2.2 of
DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and submit to the Audit Team a copy of the CY
2025 SEF Budget and the LSB Resolution/s approving the same, for verification
and audit; henceforth, strictly adhere with the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017
and succeeding issuances regarding the proper documentation, monitoring,
transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of SEF.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Instruct the City Accountant to immediately submit the DVs for the online
payments totaling P91,105,708.50;

b. Direct the Head of the Department or Office with administrative control over
the concerned fund to ensure that a DV is prepared for every payment or
settlement of government payables/obligations, regardless of the payment
method; and henceforth, strictly adhere with the disbursement process and
documentation set forth in the MNGAS for LGUs, COA Circular No. 2006-002
and related issuances, to avoid potential legal issues and recurrence of this
observation.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Direct the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO), the Human Resource Management
Office (HRMO), and the City Accounting Department to coordinate with GSIS
and reconcile City records with GSIS records for Life and Retirement
Premiums and EC. Provide the Audit Team with the reconciliation results. If the
reconciliation concludes to a payable to GSIS, prioritize payment over all other
obligations except salaries and wages of City employees to avoid interest and
penalties; and

b. Henceforth, ensure the accurate and timely remittance of contributions to the
GSIS pursuant to Section 6 of RA No. 8291, to avoid any inconvenience or
deprivation of benefits and privileges for employees from GSIS, as well as to
protect any potential legal issues and avoid unnecessary interest and
penalties.

We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Instruct the City Accountant to submit the corresponding JEVs, approved DVs, perfected contract, AIR, and/or notice of the existence of check/s pertaining to the recorded Accounts Payable amounting to P80,638,478.41 as at year-end; otherwise, make the necessary adjusting entries to revert or derecognize the Accounts Payable to ensure that subject account and other affected accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements; and b. Refrain from recognizing Accounts Payable unless the required documents to establish the validity of the claims are complete and henceforth, strictly comply with the submission of JEVs and supporting documents to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 31, Chapter 2 of the MNGAS for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant submitted the corresponding JEVs, approved DV, perfected contract and AIR pertaining to the recorded Accounts Payable amounting to P80,638,478.41 as at year-end; otherwise, make the necessary adjusting entries to revert or derecognize the Accounts Payable to ensure that subject account and other affected accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements; and refrain from recognizing Accounts Payable unless the required documents to establish the validity of the claims are complete and henceforth, strictly comply with the submission of JEVs and supporting documents to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 31, Chapter 2 of the MNGAS for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2009-006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor withdraw the funds placed under high yield savings or time deposit accounts in the LBP totaling P165,663,330.39 as of December 31, 2023, appropriate and utilize the same for the purpose for which the funds were created or given to the City and henceforth, strictly comply with RA No. 7160, COA Circular No. 92-382 and related issuances relative to time deposit accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City withdrew the funds placed under high yield savings or time deposit accounts in the LBP totaling P165,663,330.39 as of December 31, 2023, and appropriately utilized the same for the purpose for which the funds were created or given to the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (CDRRMC) to: a. Present only the amount of the available LDRRMF, consisting of the current year appropriation, the continuing appropriation and the special trust fund, if any, in the LDRRMFIP, and if practicable, revise the CY 2023 LDRRMFIP and submit the same to the Audit Team for further audit and verification; and b. Ensure significant accomplishment of the LDRRMF PPAs considering funds are available thereto, in order to enhance the preparedness and response capabilities of the City and its constituents.
observations.managementAction: a. The submitted CY 2024 LDRRMF consisted of the current year appropriation, the continuing appropriation and the special trust fund. b. In CY 2024, the City has accomplished LDRRMF PPAs considering funds are available thereto. The City implemented 31 out of the 38 planned PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Technical Working Group Chairperson and GFPS Focal Person to: a. Submit a copy of the supplemental budget covering the excess charges, if any, in coordination with the City Budget Officer; and b. Ensure significant utilization of the GAD Fund considering funds are available thereto.
observations.managementAction: The City submitted the supplemental budget covering the excess charges. In CY 2024, the City has accomplished GAD PPAs amounting to P69,040,624.80 implementing 178 PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Instruct the CGSO to:
i. follow the instructions set forth under Annex 49 of the Manual in
accomplishing the RPCPPE;
ii. after effecting aforesaid changes, submit the revised RPCPPE of the City as
of December 31, 2023 to the Audit Team for further verification and audit;
b. Instruct the City Accountant to prepare necessary accounting entries to
reclassify the semi-expendable properties amounting to P461,900.00 from PPE
account to the appropriate semi-expendable property account; and
c. At the earliest and practicable period, initiate the one-time cleansing of PPE
balances of the City by following the guidelines and procedures on the six
activities prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 enumerated below, to
eliminate any discrepancies or deviations between accounting and property
records and to establish an accurate and verifiable as to existence, condition
and accountability PPE balances:
1. Preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking;
2. Inventory taking;
3. Reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting
records;
4. Disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs;
5. Procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without
available record of accountability; and
6. Accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Instruct the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the corresponding
JEVs, approved DV, perfected contract and AIR pertaining to the recorded
Accounts Payable amounting to P80,638,478.41, otherwise, make the necessary
adjusting entries to revert or derecognize the Accounts Payable to ensure that
subject account and other affected accounts are fairly presented in the financial
statements; and
b. Refrain from recognizing Accounts Payable unless the required documents to
establish the validity of the claims are complete and henceforth, strictly comply
with the submission of JEV and supporting documents to the Audit Team
pursuant to Section 31, Chapter 2 of the Manual and COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the City Mayor withdraw the funds placed under high yield
savings or time deposit accounts in the LBP totaling P165,663,330.39 as of
December 31, 2023, and appropriate and utilize the same for the purpose for which
the funds were created or given to the City and henceforth, strictly comply with RA
No. 7160, COA Circular No. 92-382 and related issuances relative to time deposit
accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Ensure that the APP of the City for CY 2024 and succeeding years are in
accordance with the requirements on the preparation of the APP under GPPB
Circular No. 07-2015 and bear approval of the HoPE following Section 7.2 of the
2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, and submit the same to the COA Audit Team
as accompanying document to the Annual Budget;
b. Ensure 100 percent accomplishment of procurement activities considering
funds are available thereto; and
c. Instruct the BAC to report all bid results, regardless of modality, in the Report
of Bid Results submitted and uploaded in the FDPP and henceforth, strictly
adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related
issuances regarding procurement and the preparation of relevant plans and
documents.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Subject all deliveries of all items for acceptance first by the CGSO before
inspection to ensure proper and accurate recording, inventory and monitoring;
and
b. Duly notify the COA Audit Team of the time and scheduled delivery date of all
purchases of the City within 24 hours from such acceptance and submit the
corresponding report of inspection thereafter; and henceforth, strictly comply
with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 92-386 and 95-006 and the MNGAS
relative to the acceptance and inspection of the purchases of the City and the
notification of delivery and submission of the report of inspection to COA.

We recommended that the City Mayor, through the Head of the CHRMO, expedite
the hiring and/or promotion of qualified personnel to reduce, if not totally eliminate,
the need to appoint casual and JO workers, thus ensuring a more efficient and
effective government service thru strategic management of human resources.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Council to:

a. Present only the amount of the total available LDRRMF, consisting of the current
year appropriation, the continuing appropriation and the special trust fund, if
any, in the LDRRMFIP, and if practicable, revise the CY 2023 LDRRMFIP and
submit the same to the Audit Team for further audit and verification;
b. Ensure 100 percent accomplishment of LDRRMF PPAs considering funds are
available thereto, in order to enhance the preparedness and response
capabilities of the City and its constituents.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GFPS Technical Working Group
(TWG) Chairperson and GFPS Focal Person to:

a. Submit a copy of the portion of the supplemental budget covering the excess
charges, if any, in coordination with the City Budget Officer; and
b. Ensure 100 percent utilization of the GAD Fund considering funds are available
thereto.

observations.managementAction:
The City has significantly reduced the discrepancy between the accounting and the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2023 from P8,394,492.61 of previous year to P16,782.02.
No action taken by the Management.
No action taken by the Management.
The City has yet to avail the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances following COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the second semester of CY 2024.
observations.reason:
Review of the RPCPPEs as of December 31, 2023 disclosed that properties totaling P261,123,359.89 embodied in the RPCPPE-SEF were still presented on per whereabout basis or where they were located, in this case the names of the City’s schools, instead of on per type of PPE basis.
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 39-42.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the CY 2023 LDRRMFIP or Local Expenditure Program the projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF were incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant, to closely monitor the implementation of projects with funding from the continuing appropriation and consider a thorough review thereof during the annual budget preparation to determine whether there is a need to revert and/or reprogram the funds, subject to the approval of the Sanggunian Bayan upon the Mayor’s recommendation and compliance with other pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: On February 28, 2024, the City Disaster Risk Management Council issued Resolution No. 001, series of 2024, appropriating additional LDRRMF sourced from the unexpended balance of previous years’, such as the continuing appropriation and the special trust fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: direct the Municipal Accountant to pass the necessary journal entry to correct the balances of the affected accounts. Further, require him to record the proceeds from the sale of bidding documents and fees collected from other procurement activities as trust receipts to be used exclusively for the payment of honoraria and overtime pay of the Bids and Awards Committee Members, BAC-Technical Working Group and BAC-Secretariat, and treat any excess amount as income of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: On February 28, 2024, the City Accountant prepared Journal Entry Voucher No. 2023-100-12-10 dated December 29, 2023 correcting the balances of the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Budget Officer to stop the practice of including the budget for the payment of BAC honoraria in the current year appropriation of the General Fund and instead source the amount therefor from the collections from the sale of the bidding documents and savings from the budget of the Municipality, subject to the pertinent provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 and RA No. 7160; and
b. the Municipal Accountant and BAC Members, together with the BAC-TWG and BAC Secretariat, to strictly observe the procedures and funding sources in the payment of honoraria.
observations.managementAction: The City has stopped the practice of including the budget for the payment of BAC honoraria in the current year appropriation of the General Fund and instead source the amount therefor from the collections from the sale of the bidding documents and savings from the budget of the Municipality, subject to the pertinent provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 and RA No. 7160. The City now observes the procedures and funding sources in the payment of honoraria in accordance with Sections 3.1, 3.2 and 3.5 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to pass the Adjusting Journal Entries
recommended by the COA Audit Team in Appendices 7 and 8 and other
necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts in the
books of the Municipality, and henceforth observe the proper accounting
treatment, classification and recording of PPE provided under COA Circular No.
2015-009 and the capitalization threshold under COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
b. instruct the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to also reflect the adjustments in the
Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and
Property/Equipment Cards.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct both the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to review the lists of
supplies and materials in Appendices 9 and 10 which were determined by the COA
Audit Team to have been misclassified as PPE and make the necessary
adjustments in the accounting records and RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct all Inventory Custodians to ensure that all issuances of supplies and
materials are covered by RIS and the Municipal Treasurer/General Services
Officer to use the RIS as basis for the preparation of the SSMI;
b. instruct the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer to submit the SSMI to
the Municipal Accountant together with the original copies of the RIS which
will be the basis of the latter to record in the books the expended portions of
supplies and materials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper accounting treatment,
classification and recording of procurement of medical, dental and laboratory
supplies, laptop computers and printers, tables, chairs and televisions, and
medical equipments in accordance with The Revised Chart of Accounts
provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009 and COA Circular No. 2020-006;
b. require the Municipal Accountant to pass the Adjusting Journal Entries
provided in Appendix 11 recommended by the COA Audit Team, and other
necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the affected
accounts; and
c. instruct the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer to also reflect the
adjustments in the Medical Equipment and Information and Communication
Technology Equipment accounts in the Report of Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and their respective Equipment Cards.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
deduct and withhold the Final VAT and Income Tax on the payments of postpaid
plans to Globe Telecom Inc. pursuant to BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-
2014 and Revenue Regulations No. 2005-16, and remit the same to the BIR within
the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant that all Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) of the Municipality are properly identified, accounted and recorded in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009. Ensured double checking to arrive the appropriate account to be used in case there was a transfer of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor ensured that all of the Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) of the Municipality are identified, accounted and recorded in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of welfare goods and the distributed portions to Welfare Goods Expenses and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for all welfare goods; and
b. Municipal Disaster Risk Management Officer/General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards to record the purchases and issuances of welfare goods. The issuances of these items should be supported with the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and reported weekly to the Accounting Office thru the preparation and submission of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) as basis of the City Accountant in recording issued items under account Welfare Goods Expenses.
observations.managementAction: The excel files charged against the appropriation for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and the portion to Welfare Goods Expenses. The excel files stock cards and supplies ledger cards and monitoring file were immediately forwarded. Submitted immediately the necessary documents like requisition issue slip needed in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Disaster Risk Management Officer (MDRRMO) to submit to the Audit Team the disbursements of funds of LDRRMF covering all COVID-19 related programs, projects and activities to avoid suspension of the LDRRMFIP. Henceforth, it is recommended that the disbursements of LDRRMF out of the STF are supported with LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: No available LDRRMFIP. Issued Municipal Resolutions with reference numbers 12-2020 and 13-2020 to support the disbursements of LDRRMF to the Audit Team. Management officer (MDRRMO) to submit the disbursements of funds. Management council has decided and immediately proposed to allocate certain amount of the prior years’ unexpended disaster fund. In CY 2021, the STF were already included in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to coordinate with concerned Barangay officials to facilitate the submission to the COA of the updated contact numbers of identified eligible beneficiaries and attached copies of liquidation reports received by DSWD Regional Office No. IV-A on July 2, 2020.
observations.managementAction:
Attached to each claim the pictures of the recipients taken during the distribution of the subsidies to the identified eligible families within the Municipality. However, the Municipality recommended that the Municipal Management for its full compliance on the receipt and timely distribution of the SAP and the submission of attached copies of liquidation reports received by DSWD Regional Office No. IV-A on July 2, 2020. Concerned Barangay officials to facilitate the Audit Team’s further confirmation.
Management submitted copies of liquidation reports received by DSWD Regional Office No. IV-A on July 2, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) of the Municipality are properly identified, accounted and recorded in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of welfare goods under the account Welfare Goods for Distribution and the distributed portion to Welfare Goods Expenses. He should also maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for all welfare goods; and
b. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer/General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards to record the purchases and issuances of welfare goods. The issuances of these items should be supported with the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and reported weekly to the Accounting Office thru the preparation and submission of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) as basis of the City Accountant in recording issued items under account Welfare Goods Expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) to submit to the Audit Team the LDRRMC’s approved LDRRMFIP covering all COVID-19 related programs, projects, and activities to avoid suspension of the transactions. Henceforth, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the disbursements of LDRRMF out of the STF are supported with LDRRMC’s approved LDRRMFIP.

However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to coordinate with concerned Barangay officials to facilitate the submission to the COA of the updated contact numbers of beneficiaries, for the Audit Team’s further or expanded confirmation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and investigate the cause/s of the deficiencies of balances of said receivables in their respective records and make the necessary adjustments in their records and thereby henceforth, ensure that the balances of said receivables in their respective records are always equal.
observations.managementAction: Proper journals entries were now made in order to reflect the correct movement of the RPT receivables account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit to the COA the necessary supporting documents to facilitate the write-off of the dormant receivables dated February 16, 2021 and Joint Affidavit dated February 5, 2021; b. exert effort to collect the remaining loans receivable accounts by locating pertinent files, gathering the relevant information on the whereabouts or status of the debtors and sending demand letters, among others. If these efforts prove futile, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission to the COA of the request for authority to write-off the remaining dormant receivables amounting to P357,500.00, together with the necessary documents; and c. provide allowance for impairment on expected losses, taking into consideration the age of receivables, results of demand efforts and overall possibility of collection for proper valuation of receivables pursuant to Paragraph 67 of PPSAS 29.
observations.managementAction:
The total amount was requested for write-off via Municipal Resolution No.07-2021 dated February 16, 2021 and Joint Affidavit dated February 5, 2021.
An allowance for impairment was also set-up on amounting to P1,934,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all programs/projects/activities (PPAs) of the Municipality are properly identified and aligned with the Revised Chart of Accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2015-09 dated December 01, 2015 to avoid understatement/overstatement/misclassification of expense accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget and Accounting Office will also continue to ensure that all programs/projects/activities of the Municipality are properly identified and aligned with the Revised Chart of Accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2015-09 dated December 1, 2015 in order to avoid misstatement of expense accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) of the Municipality are properly identified and aligned with the Revised Chart of Accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015 to avoid understatement of the appropriate expense accounts and overstatement of the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses.
observations.reason: There were still expenses recorded as OMOE amounting to P21,365,814.55 that could be categorized under other specific expense accounts. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to analyze and investigate the cause/s of the net deficiencies
of P47,096,591.60 between balances of the RPT and SET Receivables and the List
of Delinquent Taxpayers as of December 31, 2019, make the necessary adjustments
in their records and henceforth, ensure that the balances of said receivables in their
respective records are always equal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit to the COA the necessary supporting documents to facilitate the write-
off of the dormant receivables amounting to P1,576,500.00 as enumerated in
Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005;
b. exert effort to collect the remaining loans receivable accounts by locating
pertinent files, gathering relevant information on the whereabouts or status of
the debtors and sending demand letters, among others. If these efforts prove
futile, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate the submission to the COA of the request for authority
to write-off the remaining dormant receivables amounting to P357,500.00,
together with the necessary documents; and
c. provide allowance for impairment on expected losses, taking into consideration
the age of receivables, results of demand efforts and overall possibility of
collection for proper valuation of receivables pursuant to Paragraph 67 of
PPSAS 29.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and
the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) of
the Municipality are properly identified and aligned with the Revised Chart of
Accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015 to
avoid understatement of the appropriate expense accounts and overstatement of
the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that: a. the Municipal Treasurer, who also acts as the designated inventory Goods and Supplies Officer, being the supplies custodian to prepare the monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to the accounting office as basis for the recording of inventory; b. the Municipal Accountant record the supplies and materials to the appropriate expense accounts only upon issuance supported by the monthly SSMI; and c. the Municipal Treasurer initiate the conduct of physical inventory every semester and submission of a report thereon using the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) so that discrepancies and the costs of supplies and materials actually issued/consumed can be accurately determined and taken up in the books for reconciliation and adjustment based on actual count of inventories.

We recommended that the MDRRMO: a. prepare the MDRRM Plan on the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 allocating the 30 percent of the MDRRMF in lump-sum for Quick Response Fund; b. ensure that plans, programs and Projects and Activities (PPAs) in the DRRMP of the Municipality are properly planned and feasible and timely, taking into consideration the implementation of the Programs, Projects and Activities that the projects are timely; and c. maximize the utilization of the MDRRM Fund for PPAs by seeing to it that said PPAs are implemented as planned/scheduled.
observations.managementAction:
a. The correction presentation of the MDRRM fund was made and prepared in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.
b. PPAs in the MDRRM Plan are carefully planned and implemented as timely indicated in the MDRRM plan.
c. Regularly monitors the utilization and implementation of the PPAs by seeing to it that said PPAs are implemented as planned/scheduled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the prescribed period of submission of financial statements/reports not later than the 14th day of February pursuant to the relevant provisions of Section 41 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2019 financial statements/reports were submitted on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. the Municipal Treasurer, who also acts as the Goods and Supplies Officer, instruct her designated supplies custodian from among her staff to prepare the monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to the Accounting Office as basis for the periodic recognition of inventory expenses; 
b. the OIC-Municipal Accountant record the supplies and materials to the appropriate expense accounts only upon issuance supported by the monthly SSMI; and
c. the Municipal Treasurer initiate the conduct of physical inventory every semester and submission of a report thereon using the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) so that discrepancies and the costs of supplies and materials actually issued/consumed can be accurately determined and taken up in the books for reconciliation and adjustment based on actual count of inventories.
observations.managementAction:
The Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) was not prepared and submitted to the Accounting Office.
Supplies and materials were still automatically recorded as expense at the ensuing month.
Physical inventory was not made every semester.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the
Municipal Accountant to see to it that all programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) of the
Municipality are properly identified and aligned with the Revised Chart of Accounts
provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015 to avoid
understatement of the appropriate expense accounts and overstatement of the Other
Maintenance and Operating Expenses.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that:

a. the Municipal Treasurer, who also acts as the Goods and Supplies Officer, instruct
her designated supplies custodian from among her staff to prepare the monthly
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to the
Accounting Office as basis for the periodic recognition of inventory expenses;
b. the Municipal Accountant record the supplies and materials to the appropriate
expense accounts only upon issuance supported by the monthly SSMI; and
c. the Municipal Treasurer initiate the conduct of physical inventory every semester
and submission of a report thereon using the Report of Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) so that discrepancies and the costs of supplies and materials
actually issued/consumed can be accurately determined and taken up in the books
for reconciliation and adjustment based on actual count of inventories.

We recommended that the MDRRMO:
a. prepare the MDRRM Plan based on the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002
allocating the 30 per cent of the MDRRMF in lump-sum for Quick Response Fund;
b. ensure that that plans, programs and activities in the MDRRM Plan are feasible and
timely, and conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of Programs,
Projects and Activities (PPAs) in the DRRMP of the Municipality; and
c. maximize the utilization of the MDRRM Fund for PPAs by seeing to it that said PPAs
are implemented as planned/scheduled.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
strictly observe the prescribed period of submission of financial statements/reports
pursuant to the relevant provisions of PD 1445.

observations.managementAction:
There were no more discrepancies between the accounting records and inventory reports.
The PPELCs and property records were updated and information were complete.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a. request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, as enumerated in the Municipal Resolution No. 48-2017, from the Commission on Audit supported by the relevant documents provided in Section 8.3 of the COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
b. instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to continue establishing the existence/validity of the remaining loans receivable accounts, review the collectability of these receivables, demand payments from beneficiaries who have the ability to pay and exhaust all available remedies to enforce collection.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality requested for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, as enumerated in the Municipal Resolution No. 48-2017, from the Commission on Audit supported by the relevant documents provided in Section 8.3 of the COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
The Municipality followed the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to take up in the books the payment of the market rights and goodwill and the deposits and other fees.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant recorded the payment of deposits and other fees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and reconcile the various discrepancies in balances of some PPE items between the accounting records and inventory reports and make the necessary adjustments and regularly reconcile the result of the physical count with the property and accounting records; and
OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to update and complete PPELCs and property records, respectively, of all property items by providing complete and necessary information on both records to facilitate verification and reconciliation.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, as enumerated in the Municipal Resolution No. 48-2017, from the Commission on Audit supported by the relevant documents provided in Section 8.3 of the COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to continue establishing the existence/validity of the remaining loans receivable accounts, review the collectability of these receivables, demand payments from beneficiaries who have the ability to pay and exhaust all available remedies to enforce collection.

We recommended that:
the Municipal Treasurer instruct her designated supplies custodian from among her staff to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to the Accounting Office as basis for the periodic recognition of inventory expenses;
the OIC-Municipal Accountant record the supplies and materials to the appropriate expense accounts only upon issuance supported by the monthly SSMI; and
the Municipal Treasurer initiate the conduct of physical inventory every semester and submission of a report thereon using the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI)  so that discrepancies and the costs of supplies and materials actually issued/consumed can be accurately determined and taken up in the books for reconciliation and adjustment based on actual count of inventories.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the payment of market rights and goodwill of P763,521.17 and the deposits and other fees of P342,520.00, as follows:
Other Payables					P763,521.17
Receipts from Market Operations		P763,521.17
Other Payables					P342,520.00
Customer’s Deposits				P342,520.00

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to analyze and reconcile the discrepancy of P685,191,512.38 between the accounting and assessor’s records and make the necessary adjustments; Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Assessor identify all the land owned by the Municipality to establish which are not yet recorded in the books; Municipal Assessor to determine which real properties are not yet registered in the name of the Municipal Government to establish ownership over the land; and Municipal Assessor and/or other concerned local officials facilitate the titling of real estate owned by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant has already coordinated with the Municipal Assessor to reconcile the discrepancy and the Land account was adjusted accordingly. Municipal Accountant based on the assessed value.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete physical inventory of all assets of the Municipality to determine its actual existence and the person responsible/accountable thereto and to use the RPCPPE in the inventory of PPE as prescribed by the MNGAS for LGUs; Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and reconcile the discrepancy of P510,944,335.62 between the accounting records and inventory reports and make the necessary adjustments and continuously reconcile the result of the physical count with the property and accounting records; and Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to update and complete property records and PPELCs of all property items by providing complete and necessary information on both records to facilitate verification and reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer already conducted the actual physical inventory to facilitate reconciliati on. The OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer have already reconciled most of the discrepancy, however, there was still a balance of unreconciled amount of P2,357,081.68 as of December 31, 2017. Reiterated in Finding No. 1 - Part II of this report.
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer have already reconciled most of the discrepancy, however, there was still a balance of unreconciled amount of P2,357,081.68 as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant establish the existence/validity of the loans receivable accounts and review their collectability.  For accounts deemed uncollectible, request authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off dormant accounts guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.
observations.reason: The Sangguniang Bayan passed the MunicipalResolution No. 48-2017, dated December 18, 2017, to request for the writing off of some of the dormant/non-moving receivable accounts. Despite that, the Office of the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) has not yet received any request for authority to write-off the particular dormant receivable accounts to date. Reiterated in Finding No. 2 - Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: Municipal Treasurer to use the information gathered from the list of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the Municipality Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the determination of income levels of various business establishments for purposes of calculating local business tax; and Municipal Treasurer to enforce the collection of underpayment of business tax of P139,658.92 including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the Municipal Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Resolution No. 13-2017 was passed by the Sangguniang Bayan on June 27, 2017 authorizing the Municipal Accountant to automatically deduct Municipal Tax from any business establishments with business address in Carmona, Cavite and transacting business with the Municipal Government of Carmona. The underpayment of business tax amounting to P139,658.92 was already collected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Engineer to submit the basis of computation of the ABC and written justification for non-compliance with the provisions of Section 114 (C) of the National Internal Revenue Code (NIRC) of 1997 and RR No. 16-2005 and RR No. 4-2007, as amended by RR No. 4-2007 dated September 1, 2005 and February 7, 2007, respectively. Henceforth strictly comply with pertinent provisions of the National Internal Revenue Code and Department Order No. 22 in the correct computation of the ABC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has submitted the basis of computation of the ABC and has complied with provisions of the National Internal Revenue Code and Department Order No. 22 in the correct computation of the ABC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) limit the utilization of the SEF budget only to programs, projects and activities defined under DECS-DBM-DILG JC Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B.  We also recommended to cause the restoration of the amounts inappropriately charged against SEF from the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant has already adjusted and transferred the SEF expenses to the General Fund amounting to P817,695.01.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete physical inventory of all assets of the Municipality to determine its actual existence and the person responsible/accountable thereto and to use the RPCPPE in the inventory of PPE as prescribed by the MNGAS for LGUs;
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and reconcile the discrepancy of P510,944,335.62 between the accounting records and inventory reports and make the necessary adjustments and continuously reconcile the result of the physical count with the property and accounting records; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to update and complete property records and PPELCs of all property items by providing complete and necessary information on both records to facilitate verification and reconciliation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant establish the existence/validity of the loans receivable accounts and review their collectability.  For accounts deemed uncollectible, request authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off dormant accounts guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to use the information gathered from the list of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the Municipality Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the determination of income levels of various business establishments for purposes of calculating local business tax; and
Municipal Treasurer to enforce the collection of underpayment of business tax of P139,658.92 including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the Municipal Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Engineer to submit the basis of computation of the ABC and written justification for non-compliance with the provisions of Section 114 (C) of the National Internal Revenue Code (NIRC) of 1997  and RR No. 16-2005, as amended by RR No. 4-2007 dated September 1, 2005 and February 7, 2007, respectively. Henceforth strictly comply with pertinent provisions of the National Internal Revenue Code and Department Order No. 22 in the correct computation of the ABC.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) limit the utilization of the SEF budget only to programs, projects and activities defined under DECS-DBM- DILG JC Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B.  We also recommend to cause the restoration of the amounts inappropriately charged against SEF from the General Fund.
